
Two arXiv papers from the RSS scout meet ≥3 inclusion criteria with strong signal. Grok social signals are deprioritized due to suspicious/fabricated tweet URLs and stale arXiv IDs (2409.x). Top picks: a typed semantic memory architecture for long-horizon agents, and a heterogeneous multi-agent organizational framework grounded in real-world company structure.

## Suggestions

- [Memanto: Typed Semantic Memory with Information-Theoretic Retrieval for Long-Horizon Agents](https://arxiv.org/abs/2604.22085) — Proposes a typed semantic memory system with information-theoretic retrieval designed for long-horizon agentic tasks, addressing state management, execution-time memory access, and iterative refinement across extended agent trajectories.
  Section: Research Papers | Criteria: Architectural guidance for goal-directed agent systems (patterns, orchestration, state management), Tool-use and execution design across agent lifecycle stages (planning, execution, feedback, recovery), Iterative refinement practices (evaluation loops, human-in-the-loop, observability)

- [From Skills to Talent: Organising Heterogeneous Agents as a Real-World Company](https://arxiv.org/abs/2604.22446) — Presents an organizational framework for structuring heterogeneous multi-agent systems using real-world company hierarchies, with architectural patterns for role specialization, agent orchestration, and collaborative task execution.
  Section: Research Papers | Criteria: Architectural guidance for goal-directed agent systems (patterns, orchestration, state management), Tool-use and execution design across agent lifecycle stages (planning, execution, feedback, recovery), Empirical or practical grounding (case studies, benchmarks, production deployment evidence)
